contextBlurb: Quebec's housing policy landscape includes the Programme d'habitation abordable Québec (PHAQ), which funds affordable and highly prefabricated multi-unit housing, and Bill 20 (An Act to facilitate access to housing), which amends the Act respecting the Société d'habitation du Québec to update rules on affordable housing eligibility and compensation. The Tribunal administratif du logement (TAL) oversees landlord-tenant disputes, including potential punitive damages in cases of insalubrity and insecurity. Quebec also operates rent supplement programs and subsidized housing allowing low-income households to pay 25% of their income in rent.

legislation.title: Subsidized Housing Program (Low-Rental Housing / HLM)
legislation.summary: Provincial program allowing low-income households to pay rent equal to 25% of their income through low-rental housing (HLM) units administered by the SociÃ©tÃ© d'habitation du QuÃ©bec.

legislation.title: RÃ¨gles relatives au bail rÃ©sidentiel â€” Tribunal administratif du logement
legislation.summary: Requires use of the Tribunal administratif du logement standard lease form for all new residential leases, including rooms, apartments, and houses, with mandatory spousal consent for family residences.

legislation.title: Programme d'aides financiÃ¨res pour le logement
legislation.summary: Financial assistance framework for low-income households and those with specific housing needs, administered through the SociÃ©tÃ© d'habitation du QuÃ©bec.

legislation.title: An Act to facilitate access to housing and to amend the Act respecting the Société d'habitation du Québec
legislation.summary: Amends the Act respecting the Société d'habitation du Québec to add loan guarantees and update rules on compensation for lessees who cease to be eligible for affordable or low-rental housing.

legislation.title: Programme d'habitation abordable Québec – Volet 3 : Initiative de multilogements hautement préfabriqués
legislation.summary: A component of the Programme d'habitation abordable Québec targeting organizations wishing to partner with a design-builder selected by the SHQ to develop highly prefabricated multi-unit housing projects.

legislation.title: Reconnaissance d'un propriétaire de logements étudiants
legislation.summary: Establishes a recognition process for landlords of student housing, requiring that after the first five years of recognition, more than half of the units must be rented to students.

legislation.billCode: Droits et obligations – Locateurs et locataires
legislation.title: Règles relatives aux droits et obligations des locateurs et des locataires
legislation.summary: Sets out the rights and obligations of landlords and tenants in Quebec, including rules on access to dwellings, property visits, and resolution of noise disputes between tenants.

legislation.title: Subsidized Housing Program – Québec Rent Supplement Program
legislation.summary: Allows low-income households to pay rent equal to 25% of their income through units in the private rental market or owned by housing cooperatives and non-profit housing organizations.

legislation.title: Guide du PRACIM 2025-2028 – Volet 1 : Projets de bâtiments de base sans mise en commun
legislation.summary: Sets out the framework for the PRACIM 2025–2028 program for basic building projects without shared facilities, where the benefit is directed to a single recipient organization.

legislation.title: Initiative de multilogements hautement préfabriqués – Programme d'habitation abordable Québec
legislation.summary: A component of the Programme d'habitation abordable Québec that partners housing organizations with a design-builder selected by the SHQ to develop highly prefabricated multi-unit residential projects.

legislation.title: Programme de renouvellement des actifs en culture et infrastructures municipales – Volet 1 projets de bâtiments de base sans mise en commun
legislation.summary: A 2025–2028 infrastructure program guiding the construction of basic building projects without shared facilities, applicable to single-beneficiary municipal infrastructure projects.

legislation.title: Reconnaissance d'un propriétaire de logements étudiants
legislation.summary: Establishes a recognition process for owners of student housing buildings, requiring that after five years more than half of the units must be rented to students, with owners submitting applications to the minister.

legislation.title: Droits et obligations des locateurs et des locataires
legislation.summary: Establishes the rules governing landlord and tenant obligations regarding access to housing, property visits, noise disturbances, and dispute resolution procedures in Quebec.

legislation.title: Subsidized Housing – Québec Rent Supplement Program
legislation.summary: Allows low-income households to pay rent equal to 25% of their income through housing units in the private rental market or owned by cooperatives and non-profit organizations.

legislation.title: Loi Ã©dictant la Loi visant Ã  favoriser l'accÃ¨s au logement et modifiant diverses dispositions
legislation.summary: Establishes a centralized rental application portal (guichet) for affordable and low-rental housing, and sets rules governing the allocation and leasing of dwellings in affordable housing.

legislation.title: An Act to enact the Act to facilitate access to housing and to amend various provisions
legislation.summary: English version of Projet de loi nÂ° 20; establishes rules for allocation and leasing of affordable housing dwellings and allows the filing of applications through a centralized portal.

legislation.title: Analyse d'impact rÃ©glementaire du projet de loi Ã©dictant la Loi visant Ã  favoriser l'accÃ¨s au logement
legislation.summary: Regulatory impact analysis laying the groundwork for the centralized housing application portal (guichet) for low-rental and affordable housing units in Quebec.

legislation.title: Projet de loi Ã©dictant la Loi visant Ã  favoriser l'accÃ¨s au logement et modifiant diverses dispositions â€” MÃ©moire au Conseil des ministres
legislation.summary: Council of Ministers memorandum detailing regulatory modifications required to implement the centralized housing portal, including eligibility conditions for affordable and low-rental housing.

legislation.title: Échanges entre le Ministère et le Tribunal administratif du logement – Propositions de réforme 2025
legislation.summary: Proposes granting the Tribunal administratif du logement the power to award punitive damages against landlords in cases of insalubrity and insecurity, and to anonymize tenant applications.

legislation.title: Projet de loi constitutionnelle de 2025 sur le Québec
legislation.summary: Proposes a Constitution for Quebec to protect the Quebec nation, its values, and its collective rights, with potential implications for housing and land-use jurisdiction.

legislation.title: Projet de loi visant à accélérer l'octroi des autorisations requises pour la réalisation des projets prioritaires et d'envergure nationale
legislation.summary: Aims to reduce delays in obtaining authorizations for large-scale and nationally significant projects, including housing construction, in the context of rising costs and economic uncertainty.

legislation.title: Projet de loi omnibus 2025 – Analyse d'impact réglementaire (allègement réglementaire et administratif)
legislation.summary: An omnibus regulatory relief bill for 2025 that proposes administrative simplifications including streamlined record-keeping requirements, estimated to generate recurring annual savings for businesses in Quebec.

legislation.title: Analyse d'impact réglementaire – Projet de règlement modifiant les critères du ministère des Affaires municipales et de l'Habitation
legislation.summary: The Ministère des Affaires municipales et de l'Habitation applies the Government Policy on Regulatory Streamlining to its regulatory process as outlined in the Strategic Plan 2023–2027, including amendments to housing-related regulations.
